hard.next_step: Confidence 10% 3rd of July more than 6 months the customer in silent mode , email outreach for meeting intro and to evaluate this opportunity 6th April - Have paused the project due to the war, but the ceasefire is giving confidence to restart in early-mid May when all will be back to the office - current situation is that the EMEA HQ 'may' insist on a solution used elsewhere, which could be us (US & Australia) or alternative(s) (not clear who) - next step is to share a process flow that we will need to run a live custom demo against, also interested in getting a high-level ROI analysis (not a full Value Assessment) - intention is for DQ to spend some time with project lead and CPO to build this out at the face-to-face demo session in May. 28th Jan - Provided a high-level budgetary estimate only heavily caveated - likely to be lower in scale in reality as only a small team, have nudged again to get the demo back on track before Ramadan/QBR trip. 20th Jan - Responded that its early stages, don't have the data to complete the tables, but want a high level estimate, suggest call to discuss and confirm demo dates. 9th Jan - Interested in a budgetary estimate, shared the estimation tables and requested a call/meeting to run through 8th Jan - Discovery call held with Vinoth (HOP) and two colleagues - positively received (only gap is market analysis - they are using Beroe in other regions, we will need to flesh out a story around our Smart Cube 'integration') - next step is a live demo, Dan & Tanya to propose a structure, Salonee to provide date options asap. 18th Dec - Discovery call postponed to new year - 8th Jan. 17th Dec - Inbound lead requesting a demo on Tues 23rd Dec
hard.ais_why:
• On Jan 7 2026, Vinoth Kumar (Nissan ME, Head of Procurement) said in the discovery call (note from Dan Quinn): 'Key requirements include full end-to-end automation of PR to invoice processes, enhanced tail-spend visibility, AI-driven sourcing capabilities, and integrated market intelligence.' He continued: 'the system will give me an indication that based on the market intelligence report, you are now capable to run an RFQ for this particular sourcing so that you can benefit out of the new technology or the cost.' Buyer explicitly naming AI-driven sourcing as a mandatory requirement.
• Per the 6 Apr 2026 rep note: 'Have paused the project due to the war... EMEA HQ may insist on a solution used elsewhere, which could be us (US & Australia) or alternative(s)... next step is to share a process flow that we will need to run a live custom demo against, also interested in getting a high-level ROI analysis.' Vinoth wants a live custom demo built on his own process flow - signaling deep solution-led evaluation.
• High confidence: 2 valid Avoma meetings (nissan-me.ae domain), Jan 7 transcript with 114 notes including direct Vinoth quotes naming AI-driven sourcing and market intelligence integration. Product scope includes Merlin + Merlin Intake + Agentic AI + ANA. Project is paused due to war but the AI vision was articulated by the buyer pre-pause - AI Hungry stands.

north_star_verdict.headline: Deal has gone cold. After a positively-received 07 Jan discovery call, the project was paused on 06 Apr 2026 for the regional war, the anticipated mid-May restart never happened, and the buyer has not replied since mid-January. A new Zycus RVP, Mohamad Alhakim, took the account on 29 Jun and emailed Vinoth and Salonee on 03 Jul, still unanswered. No demo has been delivered, no economic buyer is mapped, EMEA HQ may override the local choice, and the buyer himself called the whole project a nice to have. A 31 Aug close for an enterprise S2P deal that has not left Qualified is not credible.
north_star_verdict.math: 56 days to close (31 Aug 2026). Created 07 Jan 2026, roughly 180 days old and still in Qualified with no progression to Formal Evaluation, Shortlisted, or beyond. Last genuine two-way buyer exchange was the buyer email replies of 12-13 Jan 2026; every touch since (10 Feb, 13/25 Mar, 08/25 May, 18 Jun, 03 Jul) is rep-initiated and unreciprocated. Project formally paused 06 Apr 2026; promised mid-May restart did not occur. To hit 31 Aug the deal would need re-engagement, a live demo, RFI/RFP, shortlisting, validation, and contracting inside 8 weeks from a six-month cold start, which is not realistic.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Omit from forecast (Pipeline at most); no Commit or Best Case is defensible
evidence: Last buyer reply on record is the 13 Jan 2026 email; six-plus months of one-way rep outreach since (Next Step 03 Jul: 'more than 6 months the customer in silent mode').
Project paused 06 Apr 2026 due to the regional war; anticipated early-mid May restart never materialised.
New RVP Mohamad Alhakim reassigned the opp on 29 Jun 2026 and sent a reintroduction email on 03 Jul; no buyer response.
No economic buyer identified or engaged across all calls, contact roles, and fields; local budget sign-off sits with unmapped HQ/finance.
EMEA centralized process team / HQ may override the Middle East evaluation and mandate a solution used elsewhere (Next Step, 06 Apr 2026).
Vinoth Kumar framed the project as a nice to have with the current manual process working good (discovery call, 07 Jan 2026), so no compelling event exists.
Still pre-demo after ~180 days in Qualified: no RFI/RFP issued, no demo delivered, scope already halved 350k to 200k.

metrics.narrative: The pain is clear but nothing is quantified. On the 07 Jan 2026 discovery call Vinoth Kumar (Dy manager purchase) described a lean team of not more than 15 people running indirect procurement across Middle East, Egypt, Turkey and future India entirely on manual Outlook and Excel plus a standalone PO/invoicing tool called eBa, and said his single objective is I need to replace all my Outlook. He also named a specific savings lever: his European team runs monthly Beroe market-intelligence research across all commodities, and automating that market-intelligence-to-RFQ trigger would save man hours which is really good for me and would be his internal ROI selling point. However no numbers were ever put to it: on 20 Jan 2026 he said they do not have the data to complete the estimation tables and wanted only a high-level estimate, and the 06 Apr Next Step still records interest in a high-level ROI analysis (not a full Value Assessment) that was never built. Quantifying the man-hour and tail-spend savings is the outstanding move.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is mapped. On the 07 Jan 2026 call Vinoth Kumar said he personally manages sourcing for the region and that they do have secured some budgets, adding if your proposal is matching within our budget it should not be big, which positions him as the local budget holder for the indirect Middle East scope. But he was explicit that ultimate sign-off is not his alone: I need to get the finance validation, and a centralized process team based in France/Europe does the technical solution analysis for Nissan globally, with the 06 Apr Next Step warning EMEA HQ may insist on a solution used elsewhere. So the real economic authority sits with unmapped HQ finance and the European process team, none of whom have been named or engaged. Identifying and reaching that HQ/finance authority is a top move; no name should be asserted until Salesforce or the buyer supplies one.

decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer stated his evaluation criteria plainly on 07 Jan 2026. Vinoth Kumar named three pillars for the demo: a full end-to-end system showing PR to PO to invoicing that removes Outlook, how the AI helps the purchasing team, and above all how market intelligence is built into the tool. Market intelligence is the decisive and most-repeated criterion: he currently uses Beroe and Procurement IQ and wants alerts such as based on the market intelligence report, you are now capable to run an RFQ so a buyer need not check the market manually. Secondary confirmed criteria are SAP / SAP HANA integration (they are upgrading to SAP HANA) and Microsoft Teams-based intake (they use Teams). The exposed criterion is market intelligence: Tanya Rajesh confirmed the SmartCube integration is still coming up and not yet native, which is the one functional gap the buyer flagged.
sources: Avoma discovery call 07 Jan 2026 (Vinoth Kumar, Tanya Rajesh)

decision_process.narrative: Vinoth Kumar laid out the intended process on 07 Jan 2026: this call is a get to know session, followed by an official RFQ or RFI, then their internal sourcing process selects the final partner; the France/Europe process team validates the technical fit, then commercial validation against budget, then an iSupplier security check before implementation. He targeted 1 April 2026 (fiscal year start) as super ambitious with an H2 2026 fallback and confirmed Salonee Karnik would run the RFQ while he stayed lightly involved. In practice the process stalled entirely: the demo that should have opened the RFI stage never happened, the project was paused 06 Apr for the war, and no RFI/RFP has issued. So the process is understood on paper but is not being executed, and the real risk is an HQ-level override cutting across the local process the buyer described.
sources: Field history (no stage progression since 08 Jan 2026)

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are not yet in play (the deal is early, in Qualified) but the buyer volunteered a helpful signal on 07 Jan 2026: because Zycus is already deployed at Nissan Australia and the US, he does not expect big challenges in terms of security, though they will still run their iSupplier security check and data-residency validation before implementation starts. That existing Nissan footprint should materially shorten InfoSec and vendor onboarding when the deal restarts. No MSA/SOW, procurement channel, or InfoSec questionnaire has been initiated. Visibility here will come only once the demo revives the evaluation and the RFI stage begins.

identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain is well articulated and owned by Vinoth Kumar. On 07 Jan 2026 he confirmed Dan Quinn's diagnosis was pretty much in line with what we are facing today: the core problem is manual, Outlook-and-Excel-driven indirect procurement with sourcing still done in Outlook, plus tail-spend that is not focused fully (tailspins are always a headache) and head-count and workload pressure on a lean 15-person team. The sharpest, most specific pain is the absence of embedded market intelligence: his European team spends real man hours running monthly Beroe research per commodity, and he wants that automated. Notably he stressed they are 100% compliant even though it is manual and the process is working good, which is why he repeatedly called the initiative a nice to have. The pain is genuine but low-urgency, which is the deal's central weakness.

champion.narrative: There is no champion. Vinoth Kumar (Dy manager purchase, Decision Maker contact role) is the most senior and engaged buyer contact and ran the 07 Jan 2026 discovery openly and warmly, but he has shown no champion behaviour: no internal advocacy, no access offered to the HQ/finance economic buyer, no navigation of the EMEA-override risk, and he explicitly delegated day-to-day to Salonee Karnik (Purchasing Section Manager, Influencer) while staying out of the detail. Since mid-January he has gone silent through the war pause and multiple rep follow-ups. Salonee was the January scheduling contact but shows no advocacy either. What is missing to build a champion: a reason to prioritise a self-described nice to have, and someone willing to open the HQ decision-makers. Rebuilding a champion is a precondition to any commercial step.
sources: Avoma discovery call 07 Jan 2026 (Vinoth Kumar, Salonee Karnik)

competition.narrative: The primary threat is not a rival vendor but inertia. Vinoth Kumar said the project is a nice to have, they are 100% compliant even though manual, and the process is working good, and the deal has since sat idle through a war pause, so status-quo / do-nothing is the strongest current threat. SAP Ariba is the named alternative class (Competitors__c: SAP Ariba, Others; on 07 Jan Vinoth said they are looking for an end to end solution like SAP Ariba or Zycus) but there is no evidence of an active bake-off. The distinct structural threat is an EMEA HQ override: HQ may mandate a solution used elsewhere, which could be Zycus (already live at Nissan US and Australia) or an unnamed alternative, bypassing the local evaluation. Zycus's wedge is precisely that existing Nissan footprint plus the Merlin autonomous-negotiation and market-intelligence-alert story that maps directly onto Vinoth's stated ROI lever.

deal_movement.summary: The pulse is dark. The deal ran hot for one week in January (discovery call 07 Jan, budget doc and demo proposal exchanged), then cooled: amount cut 350k to 200k on 08 Jan, close date pushed from 30 Jun to 31 Aug on 16 Feb, project paused 06 Apr for the war, and no buyer reply since mid-January. Ownership moved from Dan Quinn to new RVP Mohamad Alhakim on 29 Jun, whose 03 Jul reintroduction email is unanswered.

items.change: Amount revised down from 350k to 200k, reflecting a lean indirect-only Middle East scope
Close date pushed from 30 Jun 2026 to 31 Aug 2026
Project paused due to the regional war; mid-May restart anticipated but never occurred
Opportunity reassigned from Dan Quinn to new Regional VP Mohamad Alhakim
New RVP sent a reintroduction email to Vinoth Kumar and Salonee Karnik; no reply received

competitive_position.summary: The single strongest CURRENT threat is status-quo / do-nothing: Vinoth Kumar called the project a nice to have with the manual process working good and 100% compliant (07 Jan 2026), and the deal has been dark six months, so inertia, not a vendor, is what loses this. SAP Ariba is the named alternative class (Competitors__c; buyer said an end to end solution like SAP Ariba or Zycus) but shows no active evaluation and ranks below inertia. A distinct structural risk is an EMEA HQ override (06 Apr 2026) that could mandate a solution used elsewhere over the local choice; if HQ picks Zycus we win by default, if it picks another vendor the local evaluation is moot. The eBa PO/invoicing tool plus Outlook is the incumbent stack being displaced.

competitors.name: Status quo / do-nothing (manual Outlook + eBa)
competitors.quote: It's a nice to have because today, in terms of compliance, as I said, the process is working good... we are 100% compliant.
competitors.how_we_win: Manufacture a compelling event: quantify the man-hours lost to manual Beroe market-intelligence research and tie the Merlin autonomous-negotiation/tail-spend savings to a fixed fiscal-year deadline.

competitors.name: EMEA HQ-mandated alternative (global override)
competitors.quote: EMEA HQ may insist on a solution used elsewhere, which could be us (US & Australia) or alternative(s) (not clear who).
competitors.how_we_win: Map and engage the EMEA centralized process team; use the existing Nissan US/Australia footprint to make Zycus the natural global-standard choice.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: We are looking for some end to end solution like SAP Ariba or Zycus.
competitors.source: Competitors__c: SAP Ariba; Avoma discovery call 07 Jan 2026 (Vinoth Kumar)
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with the incumbent-elsewhere reference (Zycus live at Nissan US since 2018, Australia since 2021), single-code-set fast deployment, and native SAP HANA adapters.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Zycus is a strong functional fit on the buyer's stated criteria, with one clear exposure. End-to-end automation, AI-driven sourcing/negotiation, Teams-based intake and SAP HANA integration all align; embedded market intelligence (the buyer's decisive criterion) is the one gap because the SmartCube integration is not yet native.

the system will give me an indication that based on the market intelligence report, you are now capable to run an RFQ... so that you can benefit out of the new technology or the cost.
we are going to upgrade to SAP HANA... we've developed some SAP adapters which will speed the process... 80% lower time for deployment.

items.deliverable: Share the process flow to run the live custom demo against

items.quote: I need to get the finance validation... a centralized process team based in France does the analysis.

items.quote: we do not yet have the full details required... don't have the data to complete the tables, but want a high level estimate.

items.quote: more than 6 months the customer in silent mode.

items.area: EMEA HQ / centralized process team decision-makers unmapped
items.quote: EMEA HQ may insist on a solution used elsewhere.

best_practice_check.summary: This is a cold, early-stage, cost-conscious deal whose biggest enemy is inertia and an unmapped HQ decision. The win levers are the existing Nissan footprint, a man-hour ROI attack on do-nothing, and multi-threading into HQ.
flags: Weaponise the existing Nissan footprint: Zycus is live at Nissan US (2018) and Australia (2021), Vinoth said security won't be a big challenge, and he scheduled a call with his Australian colleagues, use those references and the global-standard argument to beat both SAP Ariba and the HQ-override risk.

Break the do-nothing risk with an ROI Vinoth can sell internally: quantify the man-hours his European team loses running monthly Beroe market-intelligence research and tie it to a fixed fiscal-year deadline, he said this would be his internal selling point.
Multi-thread beyond Vinoth and Salonee into the EMEA centralized process team who validate solutions globally and may drive the override, they are entirely unmapped today.

items.name: Vinoth Kumar
items.title: Dy manager purchase
items.risk: Single point of failure and no champion behaviour; controls only local budget, delegates detail to Salonee, and has not opened the HQ economic buyer. Cooling after six-month silence.
items.sentiment: Positive but dormant: warm and open on 07 Jan, silent since mid-January.

items.name: Salonee Karnik
items.title: Purchasing Section Manager
items.risk: Thin thread; if not re-engaged the buyer side collapses to Vinoth only. Named to run the RFQ, so re-activating her is the practical path back in.
items.sentiment: Engaged in January as the RFQ/scheduling lead; no engagement since.

items.name: Ziad Baroud
items.title: Purchasing Manager
items.risk: Named Evaluator but never active on a call; unclear influence.
items.sentiment: Passive: pushed for a tight one-hour demo by email in January, no engagement since.

champion_strength.summary: No champion exists. Vinoth Kumar is the most senior engaged contact and ran the discovery openly, but shows no advocacy, no EB access, and no HQ navigation, and has been silent for six months. To build him, Zycus must give him a quantified ROI he can sell internally and a reason to prioritise a self-described nice to have.
champion_strength.source: OpportunityContactRole (Decision Maker); Avoma discovery call 07 Jan 2026; six-month buyer silence

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus is well positioned on AI for what the buyer wants: Merlin generative AI, the live autonomous-negotiation agent, and Teams-based Merlin intake all map onto Vinoth's stated appetite for AI-driven sourcing and automated market-intelligence-to-RFQ triggers. The one under-positioned area is market intelligence itself: the SmartCube integration is not yet native, which is exactly the buyer's decisive criterion.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS field reads 7.0 / Moderate. The calls support genuine AI appetite: Vinoth explicitly named AI-driven sourcing, autonomous market-intelligence-triggered RFQs, and man-hour savings as his ROI lever (07 Jan 2026), which reads slightly hotter than Moderate on vision. But it is undercut by low urgency (a nice to have) and six months of silence, so the appetite is real but not yet an active AI-led evaluation.

items.detail: No compelling event: buyer is 100% compliant manually and calls the project a nice to have, so status-quo inertia is the primary loss risk.
EMEA HQ / centralized European process team may override the local Middle East evaluation and mandate a solution used elsewhere; those decision-makers are unmapped.
Six-month buyer blackout with a 31 Aug close date; the forecast date is not credible from a cold start in Qualified.
Scope already halved 350k to 200k for a lean ~15-person indirect-only team; budget secured but undisclosed, and go-live called super ambitious with an H2 fallback.
Market intelligence (the buyer's decisive criterion) is not native: SmartCube integration is still coming up, a gap versus the buyer's Beroe / Procurement IQ expectation.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cold and one-directional. The last buyer reply was mid-January; every touch since is rep-initiated and unanswered, and the project was paused for the war with no confirmed restart.
items: Six-plus months of unanswered rep outreach (Feb through 03 Jul 2026).
Project paused 06 Apr 2026; promised mid-May restart never confirmed.
New RVP intro email 03 Jul 2026 unanswered.

scope_change.to: $200k lean, indirect-only Middle East scope for a ~15-person team
scope_change.detail: Amount was cut from 350k to 200k on 08 Jan 2026 as the picture sharpened; Vinoth described a very lean team, not more than 15 people, indirect focus only, with budget likely to be lower in scale in reality as only a small team (07 Jan 2026). A smaller, cost-conscious footprint rather than a full-suite enterprise build.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A $200k lean-team, indirect-only S2P deal that has been dark for six months: positively received on the 07 Jan discovery but paused for the regional war, never demoed, with no champion, no mapped economic buyer, an HQ-override risk, and a buyer who calls the whole project a nice to have.

win_position.text: We are the incumbent-elsewhere vendor (live at Nissan US since 2018 and Australia since 2021) and were positively received on 07 Jan, but the deal has no champion, no engaged economic buyer, and has been silent for six months.
Vinoth Kumar framed the whole project as a nice to have with the manual process working good and 100% compliant (07 Jan), so status-quo inertia, not a rival, is the biggest threat to the win.
EMEA HQ may override the local Middle East choice and mandate a solution used elsewhere (Next Step 06 Apr), and those HQ decision-makers are completely unmapped.
InfoSec is largely de-risked: Vinoth said security won't be a big challenge because Zycus is already in Nissan Australia and the US (07 Jan).

deal_momentum.text: No buyer reply since mid-January 2026; the Eid greeting (25 May), check-in (18 Jun), and new-RVP intro (03 Jul) all went unanswered.
The project was formally paused on 06 Apr for the regional war and the anticipated mid-May restart never materialised.
Still pre-demo after ~180 days in Qualified, with no RFI/RFP issued and no demo delivered.

customer_commitment.text: Buyer secured some budget but would not share it and called an April go-live super ambitious with an H2 2026 fallback (Vinoth, 07 Jan).
Only two contacts ever engaged (Vinoth Kumar, Salonee Karnik); Ziad Baroud, the named Evaluator, never appeared on a call.
No quantified ROI: buyer said we don't have the data to complete the tables (20 Jan) and wanted only a high-level estimate.

deal_risk.text: A six-month buyer blackout against a 31 Aug close date makes any forecast indefensible from a cold start in Qualified.
Scope already halved from $350k to $200k on 08 Jan for a lean ~15-person indirect-only team, a defensive, cost-conscious footprint.
The buyer's decisive criterion, embedded market intelligence, is the one functional gap: SmartCube integration was still coming up (Tanya, 07 Jan) and was never resolved.

deal_momentum.evidence: Six months of unanswered rep outreach; project paused 06 Apr; no demo, no stage progression.

customer_commitment.evidence: Nice to have framing, budget undisclosed, only two contacts engaged, no quantified ROI.

deal_risk.evidence: Cold six months against a 31 Aug close, HQ-override risk, scope halved, market-intelligence gap.

critical_signals.text: The real threat is inertia, not a vendor: Vinoth called the project a nice to have with the current manual process working good, and it has sat idle for six months.
The only substantive engagement was the 07 Jan discovery, positively received with embedded market intelligence flagged as the sole functional gap, and no demo has happened since.
An EMEA centralized process team that validates solutions for Nissan globally may override the local choice, and they are entirely unmapped.
Scope was already halved to $200k for a lean ~15-person indirect-only team, and the buyer secured but will not disclose budget, a small, cost-conscious deal.

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer was positive but non-committal: this is a get to know session to know all the solutions in the market, then an official RFQ/RFI, with the whole project framed as a nice to have; no Zycus-specific lean and six months silent.

business_case.evidence: Clear qualitative pain (manual Outlook procurement, tail-spend, monthly manual Beroe market-intelligence effort) but no numbers: buyer said we don't have the data to complete the tables (20 Jan) and wants only a high-level ROI estimate.

items.action: New RVP Mohamad Alhakim to book a short reintroduction call with Vinoth and Salonee to revive the project.

items.action: Send Vinoth a two-session demo agenda plus the SmartCube market-intelligence story on rejoining.
items.trigger: Buyer asked for a draft demo agenda and a SmartCube explanation on 07 Jan; both are overdue.
items.expected_effect: Clears the two open commitments and gives the buyer a concrete reason to reconvene, unblocking the RFI stage.

items.action: Map and reach the EMEA centralized process team and HQ finance who may drive the decision.
items.trigger: Next Step 06 Apr warns EMEA HQ may override the local choice; those decision-makers are unmapped.
items.expected_effect: Neutralises the override risk and reaches the real budget authority instead of the local budget holder only.

items.action: Build a man-hour ROI tied to replacing the European team's monthly Beroe market-intelligence effort.
items.trigger: Vinoth said automating market intelligence would be his internal ROI selling point (07 Jan).
items.expected_effect: Converts a nice to have into a funded priority by quantifying savings the champion can sell internally.

items.action: Re-confirm the buyer's fiscal-year timeline and whether the project restarts post-ceasefire.
items.trigger: Buyer targeted 1 April with an H2 fallback and paused for the war; restart never confirmed.
items.expected_effect: Establishes a realistic close date and whether to keep the deal in pipeline or disqualify.

contributions.evidence: scope narrowed vs prior — Amount was cut from 350k to 200k on 08 Jan 2026 as the picture sharpened; Vinoth described a very lean team, not more than 15 people, indirect focus only, with  (buyer likely defensive on cost or phased implementation)

commentary.win_position: Win 0 is carried by competitive strength +0.20 (weight 15); business case strength +0.30 (weight 10). Offsetting it: scope narrowed vs prior — Amount was cut from 350k to 200k on 08 Jan 2026 as the picture sharpened; Vinoth described a very lean team, not….
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 47 (roughly flat): generic demo only (from call evidence). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (0), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 47, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 2 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

bullets.text: A six-month buyer blackout against a 31 Aug close date makes any forecast indefensible from a cold start in Qualified
Scope already halved from $350k to $200k on 08 Jan for a lean ~15-person indirect-only team, a defensive, cost-conscious footprint
The buyer's decisive criterion, embedded market intelligence, is the one functional gap; SmartCube integration was still coming up (Tanya, 07 Jan) and was never resolved

blocks.footer: Competitive picture: The single strongest CURRENT threat is status-quo / do-nothing: Vinoth Kumar called the project a nice to have with the manual process working good and 100% compliant (07 Jan 2026), and the deal has been dark six months, so inertia, not a…

blocks.title: Customer commitment
blocks.read: Light — they haven't committed much yet.

bullets.text: Buyer secured some budget but would not share it and called an April go-live super ambitious with an H2 2026 fallback (Vinoth, 07 Jan)
Only two contacts ever engaged (Vinoth Kumar, Salonee Karnik); Ziad Baroud, the named Evaluator, never appeared on a call
No quantified ROI; buyer said we don't have the data to complete the tables (20 Jan) and wanted only a high-level estimate

blocks.title: What moves it forward
items: New RVP Mohamad Alhakim to book a short reintroduction call with Vinoth and Salonee to revive the project. (by 13 Jul)
Send Vinoth a two-session demo agenda plus the SmartCube market-intelligence story on rejoining. (by 20 Jul)
Map and reach the EMEA centralized process team and HQ finance who may drive the decision. (by 24 Jul)

evidence_coverage.salesforce_window: Activities last 120 days; field history trailing 365 days; contact roles current
gaps: Only one buyer call on record (07 Jan 2026 discovery); no demo or later call exists.
Economic buyer / HQ finance authority genuinely unmapped (no Economic Buyer contact role, no named EB in any field).
No quantified ROI / business case; buyer lacked data for estimation tables.
Buyer intent after the ceasefire is unknown after six months of one-way outreach.
EMEA centralized process team decision-makers who may drive a global override are unnamed.
